title: Scope sprint to the active spec with clarification guard
description: Make runSprintCommand select only the contracts whose specId matches the latest loaded spec, refuse to run when the latest spec has status needs-clarification, and improve the empty-contracts message to point at re-planning or the full pipeline. The single-spec flow stays unchanged.

successCriteria.description: Running npm run build and the typecheck completes with zero errors after the sprint-command changes.
When the contracts directory holds contracts from two different specIds, runSprintCommand executes only the contracts whose specId matches the latest spec.
When the latest spec has status needs-clarification, runSprintCommand refuses to run, prints a message naming the open clarifications, and invokes no generator.
When no contracts match the active spec, the error message instructs the user to re-plan or run the full pipeline.
The single-spec flow produces the same sprint selection as before and the full vitest suite passes.

nonGoals: Do not implement multi-spec parallel execution.
Do not change generator or evaluator behavior.
stopConditions: Stop when sprint runs only the active spec's contracts and refuses needs-clarification specs.
Stop when the single-spec flow is unchanged and all tests pass.
definitionOfDone: The sprint command scopes contract selection to the active spec, refuses to run a needs-clarification spec with a clear message, improves the empty-contracts message, and preserves the single-spec flow with all tests passing.
assumptions: loadLatestSpec (plan-state.ts:85) returns the spec whose contracts the user intends to run.
Filtering contracts by specId is safe because every materialized contract carries its specId.
outOfScope: Helper or plan-command changes (delivered in Sprints 1 and 2).
generatorNotes: In runSprintCommand (sprint.ts), after loadLatestSpec, filter listContracts to contracts where c.specId === spec.specId before findNextPendingSprint. Add a guard: if spec.status === 'needs-clarification', print the open clarification questions (reuse getOpenClarifications from contracts/spec.ts) and return without invoking the generator. Improve the existing 'No sprint contracts found' message (sprint.ts:115) to mention re-running plan or running the full pipeline. Keep the single-spec path identical when only one spec's contracts exist.
evaluatorNotes: Seed the contracts dir with two specs' contracts and assert only the latest spec's sprints run. Set the latest spec to needs-clarification and assert no generator invocation plus a clarifications message. Confirm the empty-contracts message references re-plan or run. Run the full suite to confirm no regression to the existing single-spec sprint behavior.
